title: melilotus peregrinus
intro: Melilotus peregrinus, also known as honey clover, is a perennial plant in the legume family that is native to Europe and Asia but has been naturalized in North America. The plant contains coumarin and other coumarin-like compounds, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation, acting as a diuretic, and exhibiting antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Melilotus peregrinus include reducing the risk of blood clots, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as a natural pain reliever. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
